purpose: Strict pre-implementation contracts for NVIDIA development targets. These contracts decide whether a repo is ready for a future implementation kit; they do not perform renderer edits.

contracts.name: DLSS Super Resolution / DLAA through Streamline

required_input_resources.description: Rendered color input before final presentation.
Depth buffer for the current frame.
Per-pixel motion vectors generated by the renderer.
Camera/render jitter used by temporal reconstruction.
Exposure or auto-exposure state.
Camera matrices or equivalent frame constants.

required_build_system_capabilities.description: A native renderer target that can link an SDK adapter.
A place to wire user-provided SDK include/library paths.

required_runtime_validation: Feature support query runs before UI exposure.
DLSS SR/DLAA path validates color, depth, motion vectors, jitter, exposure, camera state, and reset behavior.
Nsight or Streamline debug capture is produced before implementation readiness is claimed.
A compile command is available for the renderer target.
blockers: No native build target for SDK wiring.
unsafe_assumptions: Assuming DLSS is a generic postprocess without motion vectors, depth, jitter, and exposure.
Assuming a browser-only app can call Streamline.
Exposing DLSS UI without feature support queries.

contracts.name: DLSS Frame Generation / Multi Frame Generation readiness

required_input_resources.description: All DLSS SR temporal inputs are already available.
HUD-less/game color buffer for generated-frame interpolation.
UI or alpha/color buffer that can be composed correctly with generated frames.
Present-time frame constants and frame index.
Current and previous camera state.
Reflex or latency-marker route for FG/MFG workflows.
Explicit gates for pause, menu, loading, and resolution changes.

required_build_system_capabilities.description: A feature settings path where unsupported FG/MFG states can be hidden.

required_runtime_validation: FG/MFG is disabled during pause, menus, loading, resolution transitions, and non-gameplay frames.
Reflex/latency validation is planned before any FG/MFG UI is exposed.
Frame pacing and displayed FPS accounting are validated separately from rendered FPS.
No full FG/MFG implementation is generated from this readiness contract.
unsafe_assumptions: Treating FG/MFG as the same integration as DLSS SR.
Generating frames across menus, loading screens, or resolution transitions.

contracts.name: D3D12 DXR ray tracing base mode

required_input_resources.description: Mesh and instance data for BLAS/TLAS construction.
Material binding data for hit shaders.
G-buffer or equivalent geometry state for ray-traced mode composition.
HLSL/DXIL shader compilation path.
Render graph/pass insertion point.
A raster or non-RT fallback path.

required_build_system_capabilities.description: Shader files or shader compilation scripts can be added.

required_runtime_validation: D3D12 ray tracing feature tier is queried before enabling the mode.
BLAS/TLAS build succeeds for a representative scene.
A first ray-traced shadows/reflections pass can be captured in Nsight.
Raster fallback remains available.
unsafe_assumptions: Assuming RTX ray tracing can be bolted on without acceleration-structure ownership.
Assuming path tracing is the first base case.
Changing render graph ownership without a fallback.

contracts.name: NVIDIA Real-Time Denoisers readiness

required_input_resources.description: Noisy diffuse, specular, shadow, or occlusion signal.
Surface normal buffer.
Surface roughness/material buffer.
ViewZ or depth input.
Motion vectors for temporal denoising.
Current/previous camera matrices.
Render resolution for denoiser dispatch sizing and history state.
Reset state for cuts/resolution changes.

required_build_system_capabilities.description: Native renderer target that can link an NRD bridge.

required_runtime_validation: Denoiser selection is matched to signal type: ReBLUR, ReLAX, SIGMA, or project-specific mode.
Temporal reset behavior is tested on camera cuts and resolution changes.
Quality comparison captures before and after denoising are produced.
unsafe_assumptions: Assuming NRD can denoise without guide buffers.
Treating denoising as a generic blur pass.
Skipping reset behavior for camera cuts.

contracts.name: RTX Video SDK enhancement pipeline

required_input_resources.description: Decoded input video frame access before presentation/export.
Frame color format such as NV12, P010, RGBA, or BGRA.
8-bit and/or 10-bit bit-depth handling.
SDR/HDR and color-space metadata.
DX11, DX12, Vulkan, or CUDA native API route.
Effect route: SR, artifact reduction, SDR-to-HDR.
Output display/export surface ownership.

required_build_system_capabilities.description: Native media/video target for SDK adapter.

required_runtime_validation: Super Resolution, artifact reduction, and SDR-to-HDR are validated independently.
8-bit and 10-bit behavior is tested when supported by the local SDK.
Playback latency and throughput are measured on approved clips.
HDR output behavior is validated separately from upscaling.
blockers: Browser-only app with no native/backend path.
unsafe_assumptions: Assuming browser-only video playback can call RTX Video SDK.
Claiming HDR correctness without display/output validation.

contracts.name: NVENC/NVDEC Video Codec SDK pipeline

required_input_resources.description: Encode, decode, transcode, capture, stream, or dataset ingest goal.
Codec choice such as H.264, HEVC, or AV1.
Pixel format and chroma handling.
8-bit or 10-bit bit-depth handling.
Windows or Linux deployment target.
GPU, driver, or codec support capability check path.
Zero-copy claim validation or explicit no-claim state.
Video Codec SDK, FFmpeg, GStreamer, or Python video route.
Software or non-NVIDIA fallback path.

required_build_system_capabilities.description: A place to run throughput/quality harnesses.

required_runtime_validation: GPU codec support is checked before selecting codec/profile/bit depth/chroma.
Hardware acceleration logs prove NVENC/NVDEC use before acceleration is claimed.
Throughput, latency, PSNR/SSIM/VMAF where applicable, and A/V sync are measured.
Zero-copy claims are blocked until memory path is inspected.
blockers: No codec goal or pipeline evidence.
unsafe_assumptions: Claiming CUDA cores perform NVENC/NVDEC work.
Claiming hardware acceleration when framework logs show fallback.
